reasoning: The 10-year expected inflation rate (EXPINF10YR) dropped significantly to 2.278 on 2025-08-01, likely driven by a broader decline in market breakevens (T10YIE dropped to 2.33). Since 2.278 is an outlier low for the year (the 2025 average has hovered around 2.35), mean reversion suggests subsequent monthly prints are more likely to be higher than 2.278. P(increase) for the upcoming publication (expected around Sep 2) is ~64%, accounting for standard random walk volatility and mean-reversion pull. Following the instruction that uncertainty should increase with forecast horizon (probabilities closer to 0.5 for distant dates), the probability is reduced gradually for the November 2025 and January 2026 resolution dates.
